answer: London's population in 2024 is estimated at over 9 million. The growth is primarily due to net international migration. The latest data is from the Office for National Statistics.

results.title: ONS Population Estimates for England and Wales: Mid-2024 - SLCC
results.snippet: The population of England and Wales continued to grow in the year to mid-2024, reaching an estimated 61.8 million people (61,806,682). The size

results.title: Population estimates for England and Wales: mid-2024
results.snippet: # Population estimates for England and Wales: mid-2024. National and subnational mid-year population estimates for England and Wales by administrative area, age and sex. The size of the population increased by 706,900 (1.2%) from mid-2023. #### Figure 1: The size of the population in England and Wales continues to grow. ###### Annual population change, England and Wales, mid-1949 to mid-2024. Although estimated net international migration was lower in the year to mid-2024 compared with the previous year, it continued to contribute most to population growth. ###### Components of population change, England and Wales, year to mid-2024. We estimate that there were 13,600 net internal moves out of England and Wales to the rest of the UK in the year to mid-2024. There was a decrease in the number of births and deaths in the year to mid-2024, compared with the previous year. In both England and Wales, net international migration contributed most to population growth in the year to mid-2024.

results.title: Population estimates for the UK, England, Wales, Scotland and Northern Ireland: mid-2024 - Office for National Statistics
results.snippet: # Population estimates for the UK, England, Wales, Scotland and Northern Ireland: mid-2024 UK Statistics Authority Kitemark. National and subnational mid-year population estimates for the UK and its constituent countries by administrative area, age and sex. * Population estimates for the UK, England, Wales, Scotland and Northern Ireland: mid-2024. National and subnational mid-year population estimates for the UK and its constituent countries by age and sex. They are the official set of population estimates for the UK and its constituent countries, the regions and counties of England, and local authorities and their equivalents. * Estimates of the population for the UK, England, Wales, Scotland, and Northern Ireland. National and subnational mid-year population estimates for the UK and its constituent countries by administrative area, age and sex (including components of population change, median age and population density). Quality and methodology information for mid-year population estimates in the UK, detailing the strengths and limitations of the data, methods used, and data uses and users.
